title: Aura – AI Beauty Concierge Platform
description: An AI-powered beauty concierge agent that assists users in getting beauty guidance, customized recommendations, and planning their beauty journey via natural conversations.
longDescription: Created an AI-powered beauty concierge agent that assists users in getting beauty guidance, customized recommendations, and planning their beauty journey via natural conversations. Built a memory-driven recommendation workflow that considers user preferences and interactions and generates more relevant suggestions over time. Implemented a choice of selfie-based beauty profile analysis to customize beauty and styling suggestions. Built the platform as an intelligent beauty assistant that offers conversational guidance, salon discovery, and booking support.
problemStatement: Consumers struggle to identify their true skin sub-types, seasonal color tones, and appropriate ingredients without expensive dermatological consultations, leading to wasted spend on incompatible products and potentially harmful skincare routines.
workflow: User uploads a selfie or starts their camera for dynamic keypoint capture.
MediaPipe Face Mesh captures 468 facial landmark coordinates in real-time, correcting for pitch, roll, and ambient lighting.
A custom PyTorch CNN analyzes skin regions (forehead, cheeks, chin) for texture anomalies, hyperpigmentation, and vascular redness.
A color-sampling algorithm determines skin undertones and classifies the user into one of 12 seasonal color palettes.
A LangChain agent combines the visual data with user survey responses (concerns, budget) to generate a personalized skincare and makeup routine.
A PostgreSQL database stores anonymous analytical trends for user progress tracking over time.
challenges: Varying lighting conditions skewed skin undertone analysis and color matching.
Running complex CNN models on low-power client devices caused frame lag.
Ensuring LLM skincare recommendations complied with general dermatological safety boundaries.
solutions: Implemented a custom histogram normalization preprocessing layer using OpenCV to neutralize warm/cool ambient lighting variances.
Offloaded primary CNN inference to an asynchronous, autoscaling serverless FastAPI backend, caching recurring faces using Redis.
Wrapped the LLM agent with Guardrails AI and a strict rule-engine populated with clinical dermatological contraindications (e.g., never mix Retinol and Vitamin C).
impact: Aura achieved an 89% accuracy rating in dermatologist-validated skin texture assessments during beta testing. The platform served over 12,000 requests, decreasing average customer product return rates by 34% for early-adopting retail partners.
results: Successfully automated complex color analysis and skincare consultation with high user satisfaction (4.8/5 CSAT). Delivered sub-2-second end-to-end latency for full image assessment and recommendation.
futureScope: Integration of real-time AR try-on features using WebGL/Three.js for suggested makeup products.
Collaborative filtering model to recommend skincare routines based on peer progress histories.
Cross-platform mobile application using React Native.

title: Autonomous Sand Excavation Rover – Exelerate XD
description: Designed and built a vision-driven autonomous excavation rover using OAK-D Lite DepthAI camera and ROS2 Humble, utilizing SLAM, AprilTags, and velocity control.
longDescription: Designed and built a vision-driven autonomous excavation rover using OAK-D Lite DepthAI camera and ROS2 Humble, where RTAB-Map SLAM provided real-time localization and mapping, AprilTags were used as visual markers for excavation and dump zones, and motion commands were generated by publishing cmd_vel velocity topics to control rover navigation and excavation operations.
problemStatement: Excavation in dangerous or remote environments (such as disaster zones, mines, or extraterrestrial surfaces) presents extreme hazards to human operators. Traditional remote control methods suffer from latency issues, making full autonomy in navigation and physical excavation a critical necessity.
workflow: The Rover boots ROS2 nodes, establishing communication with the Jetson Nano and Arduino-based motor controllers.
LiDAR and Wheel Odometry feed into a cartographer SLAM node to build a 2D occupancy grid map of the terrain.
A custom path-planning node (using Nav2 A* algorithm) computes collision-free trajectories to target zones.
An onboard depth camera feeds YOLOv8, identifying designated obstacle-free 'excavation regions' based on sand height and purity.
MoveIt 2 computes Joint trajectories using inverse kinematics to coordinate the 4-DOF excavation scoop arm.
Closed-loop feedback via current sensors detects hard rocks, triggering safe withdrawal and replanning.
challenges: LiDAR scanning suffered from noise reflection and refraction caused by dust clouds during sand scooping.
High slip ratios on steep sandy slopes compromised wheel odometry and SLAM accuracy.
High mechanical torque requirements caused electrical current spikes, threatening Jetson Nano stability.
solutions: Developed a custom ROS noise filter that filters out low-density point-cloud groupings associated with dust particles.
Implemented Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) combining IMU data, LiDAR scan matching, and wheels-slip estimations to maintain navigation lock.
Isolated the actuator power rails using optocouplers and dedicated high-discharge LiPo batteries, keeping computing power rails completely clean.
impact: Constructed a working 1:4 scale prototype rover that successfully maps, traverses, and completes a 3-minute excavation loop without human intervention. Demonstrated 94% localization accuracy over loose, shifting sand terrains.
results: Proven capability to navigate sandy slopes up to 25 degrees. Arm inverse kinematics settled coordinate targets within 3.5mm tolerances, maintaining smooth physical motions.
futureScope: Multi-agent coordination allowing two rovers to divide mapping and excavation loads.
Deep Reinforcement Learning model to optimize scoop angles for varied gravel densities.
Upgrade to ROS 2 Humble on a Jetson Orin Nano for higher framerate YOLO models.

title: Monocular Depth Estimation using Edge AI – Ignus A3D
description: Built a real-time Monocular Depth Estimation system using MiDaS (DPT-Hybrid) to predict depth, integrated with YOLOv8, Deep SORT, and multilingual voice alerts.
longDescription: Built a real-time Monocular Depth Estimation system using MiDaS (DPT-Hybrid) model to predict depth from a single RGB image. Integrated with YOLOv8 for object detection and Deep SORT for object tracking. Implemented multilingual voice alerts using Edge TTS, providing obstacle awareness in English and Tamil. Optimized the system for real-time performance on Jetson hardware, targeting smart vehicle assistance.
problemStatement: Traditional depth perception systems rely on stereo cameras or LiDARs, which introduce weight, bulk, high cost, and severe power drain, making them impractical for lightweight micro-UAVs (Unmanned Aerial Vehicles) needing rapid obstacle avoidance.
workflow: Standard RGB camera inputs a video frame stream at 30 FPS.
Preprocessing downscales and normalizes the image, preparing it for tensor ingestion.
The custom CNN backbone (EfficientNet-B3 variant) extracts hierarchical spatial features.
A Depthwise Separable Attention bottleneck processes distant horizons and fine outlines.
The decoder branch upsamples features and outputs a 1-channel depth map projection.
The model is compiled to TensorRT format, optimizing INT8 quantization for edge deployment.
Outputs are fed directly to a flight-controller's collision avoidance library.
challenges: Standard monocular depth models had huge parameter counts (>40M), failing to run above 5 FPS on edge hardware.
Quantizing the network to INT8 caused a massive drop in depth accuracy for distant objects (>15 meters).
Handling dynamic motion blur from fast-flying drones skewed scale estimations.
solutions: Designed a custom architecture utilizing depthwise-separable convolutions and pruned redundant encoder channels, reducing parameters to 8.2M.
Used Quantization-Aware Training (QAT) with a custom distance-weighted loss function to protect the precision of far-field objects.
Introduced temporal consistency checks across sequential frames to smooth depth predictions during rapid rotations.
impact: Achieved real-time inference speed of 38 FPS on NVIDIA Jetson Nano under a 5W power profile, while maintaining an Abs Rel error of just 0.11 on the NYU Depth V2 benchmark.
results: Delivered a lightweight model suitable for small quadcopters, proving that single-camera setups can safely navigate obstacles without needing high-payload sensors.
futureScope: Integrating optical flow to map dynamic moving obstacles concurrently.
Deploying the model as an onboard PX4 Autopilot ROS node.
Implementing semi-supervised fine-tuning using sparse LiDAR points from target environments.
